   660	    def _run_with_num_predict_growth(
   661	        self,
   662	        runner,
   663	        *,
   664	        runner_name: str,
   665	        initial_num_predict: int,
   666	    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
   667	        """Retry a completion with a larger token budget after truncation."""
   668	        num_predict = max(1, int(initial_num_predict))
   669	        last_result: dict[str, Any] = {
   670	            "text": "",
   671	            "prompt_tokens": 0,
   672	            "completion_tokens": 0,
   673	            "total_tokens": 0,
   674	        }
   675	
   676	        for attempt in range(self._max_num_predict_retries + 1):
   677	            result = runner(num_predict)
   678	            text = self._strip_visible_think(result.get("text", "")).strip()
   679	            done_reason = str(result.get("done_reason") or "").strip().lower()
   680	            result["text"] = text
   681	
   682	            if text:
   683	                last_result = result
   684	
   685	            if done_reason != "length":
   686	                return result
   687	
   688	            if attempt >= self._max_num_predict_retries:
   689	                return last_result
   690	
   691	            next_num_predict = self._next_num_predict(num_predict)
   692	            self.logger.log_info(
   693	                f"↩️ Retrying {runner_name} after truncated response "
   694	                f"(done=length): num_predict {num_predict} -> {next_num_predict}"
   695	            )
   696	            num_predict = next_num_predict
   697	
   698	        return last_result

   702	    def list_models(self):
   703	        """List available models from Ollama."""
   704	        response = requests.get(
   705	            f"{self.service_url}/api/tags",
   706	            timeout=(self._connect_timeout, self._read_timeout),
   707	        )
   708	        response.raise_for_status()
   709	        return [
   710	            model.get("name")
   711	            for model in response.json().get("models", [])
   712	            if isinstance(model.get("name"), str) and model.get("name").strip()
   713	        ]

   715	    def list_model_details(self) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
   716	        """List available Ollama models with backend size metadata."""
   717	        response = requests.get(
   718	            f"{self.service_url}/api/tags",
   719	            timeout=(self._connect_timeout, self._read_timeout),
   720	        )
   721	        response.raise_for_status()
   722	        models: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
   723	
   724	        for item in response.json().get("models", []):
   725	            if not isinstance(item, dict):
   726	                continue
   727	
   728	            name = str(item.get("name") or "").strip()
   729	            if not name:
   730	                continue
   731	
   732	            details = item.get("details") if isinstance(item.get("details"), dict) else {}
   733	            size_bytes = item.get("size_bytes", item.get("size"))
   734	            size_mb = item.get("size_mb")
   735	            if size_mb in (None, "") and size_bytes not in (None, ""):
   736	                try:
   737	                    size_mb = int(round(float(size_bytes) / (1024.0 * 1024.0)))
   738	                except Exception:
   739	                    size_mb = None
   740	            elif size_mb not in (None, ""):
   741	                try:
   742	                    size_mb = int(round(float(size_mb)))
   743	                except Exception:
   744	                    size_mb = None
   745	
   746	            parameter_size = item.get("parameter_size") or details.get("parameter_size")
   747	            quantization_level = item.get("quantization_level") or details.get("quantization_level")
   748	            models.append(
   749	                {
   750	                    "name": name,
   751	                    "size_bytes": size_bytes,
   752	                    "size_mb": size_mb,
   753	                    "parameter_size": parameter_size,
   754	                    "quantization_level": quantization_level,
   755	                }
   756	            )
   757	
   758	        return models

   760	    def send_prompt(
   761	        self,
   762	        prompt_type: str,
   763	        prompt: str,
   764	        stream: bool = False,
   765	        generation_options: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
   766	    ) -> str:
   767	        """
   768	        FORCE think=False (hide visible chain-of-thought). For very short prompts (like "Hi"),
   769	        avoid a system prompt to match the working curl. Retry with larger budget, then fall back
   770	        to /api/generate. Always return non-empty text.
   771	        """
   772	        # Hard override: do NOT show visible reasoning
   773	        show_reasoning = False
   774	
   775	        # Heuristic: for very short inputs, match your curl exactly (no system message)
   776	        p = (prompt or "").strip()
   777	        is_very_short = len(p) <= 12  # "Hi", "Hello", etc.
   778	
   779	        result = self._run_with_num_predict_growth(
   780	            lambda num_predict: self._chat_once(
   781	                p,
   782	                show_reasoning=False,
   783	                num_predict=num_predict,
   784	                use_system=not is_very_short,
   785	                generation_options=generation_options,
   786	            ),
   787	            runner_name="/api/chat",
   788	            initial_num_predict=self._initial_num_predict(generation_options),
   789	        )
   790	        text = str(result.get("text") or "").strip()
   791	        if text:
   792	            return text
   793	
   794	        # Fallback: /api/generate with the same truncation-aware budget growth.
   795	        result = self._run_with_num_predict_growth(
   796	            lambda num_predict: self._generate_once(
   797	                p or "Say hi",
   798	                num_predict=num_predict,
   799	                generation_options=generation_options,
   800	            ),
   801	            runner_name="/api/generate",
   802	            initial_num_predict=self._initial_num_predict(generation_options),
   803	        )
   804	        text = str(result.get("text") or "").strip()
   805	        if text:
   806	            return text
   807	
   808	        # Absolute last resort: avoid empty payload to server
   809	        return "Hello! 👋"

   811	    def stream_prompt_deltas(
   812	        self,
   813	        prompt_type: str,
   814	        prompt: str,
   815	        generation_options: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
   816	        on_usage_metadata: LLMUsageMetadataCallback | None = None,

   818	        """Yield Ollama response text deltas using native HTTP streaming."""
   819	        del prompt_type

   821	        is_very_short = len(p) <= 12
   822	        yielded = False
   823	
   824	        for delta in self._chat_stream(
   825	            p,
   826	            show_reasoning=False,
   827	            num_predict=self._initial_num_predict(generation_options),
   828	            use_system=not is_very_short,
   829	            generation_options=generation_options,

   843	            if usage is not None and on_usage_metadata is not None:
   844	                on_usage_metadata(usage)

   846	            if text:

   849	    def send_prompt_with_meta(
   850	        self,
   851	        prompt_type: str,
   852	        prompt: str,
   853	        stream: bool = False,
   854	        generation_options: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
   855	    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
   856	        """
   857	        Send prompt to Ollama and return text plus token metadata.
   858	
   859	        Token counts use Ollama's prompt/eval counts. The returned total is
   860	        stored against the assistant turn for dashboard trending.
   861	        """
   862	        p = (prompt or "").strip()
   863	        is_very_short = len(p) <= 12
   864	
   865	        result = self._run_with_num_predict_growth(
   866	            lambda num_predict: self._chat_once(
   867	                p,
   868	                show_reasoning=False,
   869	                num_predict=num_predict,
   870	                use_system=not is_very_short,
   871	                generation_options=generation_options,
   872	            ),
   873	            runner_name="/api/chat",
   874	            initial_num_predict=self._initial_num_predict(generation_options),
   875	        )
   876	        text = str(result.get("text") or "").strip()
   877	        if not text:
   878	            result = self._run_with_num_predict_growth(
   879	                lambda num_predict: self._generate_once(
   880	                    p or "Say hi",
   881	                    num_predict=num_predict,
   882	                    generation_options=generation_options,
   883	                ),
   884	                runner_name="/api/generate",
   885	                initial_num_predict=self._initial_num_predict(generation_options),
   886	            )
   887	            text = str(result.get("text") or "").strip()
   888	
   889	        if not text:

   891	
   892	        return {
   893	            "text": text,
   894	            "prompt_tokens": int(result.get("prompt_tokens") or 0),
   895	            "completion_tokens": int(result.get("completion_tokens") or 0),

    88	def load_plugin_class(manifest: PluginManifest) -> type:
    89	    """Loads a plugin class from a manifest entry point.
    90

   141	    """Load a plugin class from a manifest entry point string."""

   302	    def _run_scheduled_service(self, record: _ServiceRecord) -> None:

   314	    def _run_tick(self, record: _ServiceRecord) -> None:

   381	        except (TypeError, ValueError):

   385	            kwargs = {

   388	                if key in signature.parameters

     1	"""Plugin execution orchestration for candidate plugins."""
     2	# Author: Clive Bostock
     3	# Date: 2026-04-30
     4	# Description: Tries routed plugin candidates in order and returns the first
     5	#   handled result.
     6	
     7	from __future__ import annotations
     8	
     9	from typing import Any
    10	
    11	from model.plugin_routing.handoff import PluginRoutingHandoff
    12	from model.plugin_runtime import (
    13	    PluginDataAccess,

    15	    PluginRuntimeError,
    16	    instantiate_plugin,
    17	    load_plugin_class,
    18	)
    19	
    20	
    21	class PluginRouter:
    22	    """Attempts execution for candidate plugins without owning discovery or indexing."""
    23	
    24	    def __init__(self, plugin_manager, logger, config_mgr, context_manager):
    25	        self._plugin_manager = plugin_manager
    26	        self._logger = logger
    27	        self._config_mgr = config_mgr
    28	        self._context_manager = context_manager
    29	
    30	    def route(
    31	        self,
    32	        prompt: str,
    33	        meta: dict[str, Any] | None,
    34	        handoff: PluginRoutingHandoff | None,
    35	        auth_user: str,
    36	    ) -> PluginExecutionResult | None:
    37	        """Returns the first successful plugin execution result, or None."""
    38	        if handoff is None or not handoff.candidates or self._plugin_manager is None:
    39	            return None
    40	
    41	        meta = meta or {}
    42	
    43	        for candidate in handoff.candidates:
    44	            manifest = self._plugin_manager.get_manifest(candidate.plugin_id)
    45	            if manifest is None:
    46	                self._logger.log_debug(
    47	                    f"Plugin execution skipped because manifest '{candidate.plugin_id}' was not found."
    48	                )
    49	                continue
    50	            if not manifest.entry_point:
    51	                self._logger.log_debug(
    52	                    f"Plugin execution skipped for '{candidate.plugin_id}' because no entry_point is defined."
    53	                )
    54	                continue
    55	
    56	            try:
    57	                plugin_class = load_plugin_class(manifest)
    58	                data_access = PluginDataAccess(
    59	                    manifest=manifest,
    60	                    context_manager=self._context_manager,
    61	                    auth_user=auth_user,
    62	                    logger=self._logger,
    63	                )
    64	                plugin_instance = instantiate_plugin(
    65	                    plugin_class,
    66	                    logger=self._logger,
    67	                    config_mgr=self._config_mgr,
    68	                    data_access=data_access,
    69	                )
    70	                if hasattr(plugin_instance, "can_handle") and not plugin_instance.can_handle(prompt):
    71	                    self._logger.log_debug(
    72	                        f"Plugin '{candidate.plugin_id}' declined prompt after execution-time handle check."
    73	                    )
    74	                    continue
    75	                result = plugin_instance.execute(prompt, meta)
    76	            except PluginRuntimeError as exc:
    77	                self._log_exception(f"Plugin runtime failed for '{candidate.plugin_id}' (non-fatal)", exc)
    78	                continue
    79	            except Exception as exc:
    80	                self._log_exception(f"Plugin execution failed for '{candidate.plugin_id}' (non-fatal)", exc)
    81	                continue
    82	
    83	            if result is not None and result.handled:
    84	                self._logger.log_info(f"Plugin '{candidate.plugin_id}' handled request directly.")
    85	                return result
    86	
    87	        self._logger.log_debug("No plugin candidate handled the request directly; falling back to conversational flow.")
    88	        return None
    89	
    90	    def _log_exception(self, prefix: str, exc: BaseException) -> None:
    91	        self._logger.log_error(f"{prefix}: {exc}")

    98	        unknown_fields = sorted(set(data.keys()) - ALLOWED_FIELDS)

    24	class HomeAssistantService:
    25	    """Lifecycle placeholder for the Home Assistant long-running service."""
    26	
    27	    def __init__(self, logger=None, config_mgr=None, manifest=None) -> None:
    28	        self._logger = logger
    29	        self._config_mgr = config_mgr
    30	        self._manifest = manifest
    31	        self._started = False
    32	
    33	    def run(self, context: Any) -> None:
    34	        """Run until Orac requests cancellation through the service context."""
    35	        self._started = True
    36	        if self._logger is not None:
    37	            self._logger.log_info(
    38	                "Home Assistant service placeholder started; no websocket "
    39	                "connection is attempted in this pass."
    40	            )
    41	        while not context.stop_event.wait(0.1):
    42	            pass
    43	
    44	    def stop(self, context: Any) -> None:
    45	        """Observe Orac-owned cancellation without spawning unmanaged work."""
    46	        context.stop_event.set()
    47	
    48	    def health(self, context: Any) -> bool:
    49	        """Return placeholder health based on cancellation state."""
    50	        return self._started and not context.stop_event.is_set()

    61	    def can_handle(self, prompt: str) -> bool:

   131	    def _lookup_named_location(

   159	    def _location_lookup_candidates(self, location_name: str) -> list[str]:
   160	        """Return lookup candidates from most specific to more forgiving variants."""
   161	        normalized_name = str(location_name or "").strip(" ?.!,")
   162	        if not normalized_name:
   163	            return []
   164	
   165	        candidates = [normalized_name]
   166	        country_hint = self._country_hint_from_home_location(self._home_location())
   167	        if country_hint and "," not in normalized_name:
   168	            candidates.append(f"{normalized_name}, {country_hint}")
   169	
   170	        parts = [part.strip() for part in normalized_name.split(",") if part.strip()]
   171	        if len(parts) > 1:
   172	            primary = parts[0]
   173	            qualifiers = parts[1:]
   174	            candidates.append(primary)
   175	            if country_hint:
   176	                candidates.append(f"{primary}, {country_hint}")
   177	            for qualifier in qualifiers:
   178	                candidates.append(f"{primary}, {qualifier}")
   179	
   180	        return candidates

    49	    def refresh(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
    50	        """Refreshes manifests, cache, embeddings, and the in-memory index."""
    51	        self._log_info(f"Plugin routing refresh starting for root {self._plugins_dir}")
    52	        discovered_count = len(list(self._plugins_dir.glob("*.json"))) if self._plugins_dir.exists() else 0
    53	        manifests, errors = self._discovery.discover()
    54	        valid_count = len(manifests)
    55	        invalid_count = len(errors)
    56	        enabled_manifests = [manifest for manifest in manifests if manifest.enabled]
    57	        disabled_count = valid_count - len(enabled_manifests)
    58	        runtime_manifests, dependency_disabled_manifests = self._runtime_eligible_manifests(
    59	            enabled_manifests

    63	        if disabled_count:
    64	            self._log_info(f"Plugin routing skipped {disabled_count} disabled plugin manifest(s).")

    80	        vectors_for_index: dict[str, list[float]] = {}

    84	        re_embedded = 0

   117	        self._cache.save(

   120	            plugin_entries=cache_entries_to_save,

   122	
   123	        self._index.build(vectors_for_index)
   124	        self._manifests = {manifest.plugin_id: manifest for manifest in runtime_manifests}
   125	        self._last_refresh_report = {

   128	            "discovered": discovered_count,

   131	            "enabled": len(enabled_manifests),

   146	            f"dependency_disabled={len(dependency_disabled_manifests)} "
   147	            f"cache_hits={cache_hits} cache_misses={cache_misses} re_embedded={re_embedded}"
   148	        )
   149	        return dict(self._last_refresh_report)
   150	
   151	    def find_candidates(
   152	        self,
   153	        utterance: str,
   154	        top_n: int = 5,
   155	        min_score: float | None = None,
   156	    ) -> list[PluginCandidate]:
   157	        """Returns scored plugin candidates for a user utterance."""
   158	        if not self._manifests:
   159	            self.refresh()
   160	
   161	        query_vector = self._embedding_provider.embed_text(utterance)
   162	        return self._index.search(query_vector=query_vector, top_n=top_n, min_score=min_score)
   163	
   164	    def get_manifest(self, plugin_id: str) -> PluginManifest | None:
   165	        """Returns the manifest for an indexed plugin."""
   166	        return self._manifests.get(plugin_id)
   167	
   168	    def status(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
   169	        """Returns the last refresh report."""
   170	        return dict(self._last_refresh_report)
   171	
   172	    @staticmethod
   173	    def _is_cache_hit(
   174	        manifest: PluginManifest,
   175	        canonical_text: str,
   176	        cached_entry: dict[str, Any] | None,
   177	    ) -> bool:
   178	        if not cached_entry:
   179	            return False
   180	        return (
   181	            cached_entry.get("manifest_hash") == manifest.manifest_hash
   182	            and cached_entry.get("canonical_text") == canonical_text
   183	        )
   184	
   185	    def _runtime_eligible_manifests(
   186	        self,
   187	        manifests: list[PluginManifest],
   188	    ) -> tuple[list[PluginManifest], list[PluginManifest]]:
   189	        """Return manifests eligible for on-demand routing and dependency-disabled manifests."""
   190	        eligible: list[PluginManifest] = []
   191	        dependency_disabled: list[PluginManifest] = []
   192	
   193	        for manifest in manifests:
   194	            if manifest.runtime_mode == "service":
   195	                continue
   196	            if manifest.database_required and self._has_missing_database_schema(manifest):
   197	                if manifest.database_on_missing == "fail_refresh":
   198	                    schema_names = ", ".join(
   199	                        schema.schema_name for schema in manifest.database_schemas
   200	                    )
   201	                    raise RuntimeError(
   202	                        "Plugin routing refresh failed because plugin "
   203	                        f"'{manifest.plugin_id}' is missing required database "
   204	                        f"schema metadata: {schema_names}"
   205	                    )
   206	                if manifest.database_on_missing == "warn_disable":
   207	                    dependency_disabled.append(manifest)
   208	                    continue
   209	            eligible.append(manifest)
   210	
   211	        return eligible, dependency_disabled
   212	
   213	    def _has_missing_database_schema(self, manifest: PluginManifest) -> bool:
   214	        """Return whether a manifest declares a required schema with no local bundle."""
   215	        for schema in manifest.database_schemas:
   216	            if not (self._database_schema_root / schema.schema_name).is_dir():
   217	                return True
   218	        return False

   221	        if self._logger is not None:
   222	            self._logger.log_debug(message)
   223	
   224	    def _log_info(self, message: str) -> None:
   225	        if self._logger is not None:
   226	            self._logger.log_info(message)
   227	
   228	    def _log_warning(self, message: str) -> None:
   229	        if self._logger is not None:
   230	            self._logger.log_warning(message)
   231	
   232	    def _log_error(self, message: str) -> None:
   233	        if self._logger is not None:
   234	            self._logger.log_error(message)

    37	def find_highest_release(directory: Path, file_prefix: str = 'bdds') -> Optional[Path]:
    38	    """Find the highest versioned file in a directory matching the format
    39	    '<file_prefix>-x.y.z.tar.gz'. E.g. bdds-2.3.1.tar.gz.
    40	
    41	    Args:
    42	        directory (Path): The directory to search for matching files.
    43	        file_prefix (str): The expected filename prefix (e.g. 'bdds').
    44	
    45	    Returns:
    46	        Optional[Path]: Path to the file with the highest version number,
    47	                        or None if no matching file is found.
    48	    """
    49	    # Escape prefix in case it contains regex meta characters
    50	    escaped_prefix = re.escape(file_prefix)
    51	    pattern = re.compile(rf"^{escaped_prefix}-(\d+)\.(\d+)\.(\d+)\.tar\.gz$")
    52	
    53	    highest_version = (-1, -1, -1)
    54	    selected_file = None
    55	
    56	    for file in directory.iterdir():
    57	        if file.is_file():
    58	            match = pattern.match(file.name)
    59	            if match:
    60	                version = tuple(map(int, match.groups()))
    61	                if version > highest_version:
    62	                    highest_version = version
    63	                    selected_file = file
    64	
    65	    return selected_file

    68	def is_valid_dir_name(directory_name: str) -> bool:
    69	    """

    77	    # Determine the invalid characters based on the OS

    80	        invalid_pattern = r'[\\/:*?"<>|]'
    81	    else:
    82	        # Unix-like systems restrictions: /
    83	        invalid_pattern = r'[\/]'

   124	    """Zips a directory and its contents into a zip file. If a destination_dir is provided, the generated zip file is
   125	    relocated to destination_dir.
   126	
   127	      :param zip_source_dir: The path to the directory to zip.
   128	      :type zip_source_dir: Path
   129	      :param zip_file_name: The path to the output zip file.
   130	      :type zip_file_name: str
   131	      :param destination_dir: The place to locate the generated zip file.
   132	      :type destination_dir:
   133	    """
   134	
   135	    # The make_archive method automatically adds a .zip extension. So we remove it.
   136	    _zip_file = zip_file_name.replace('.zip', '')

   138

    18	class ConfigManager:
    19	    """
    20	    Load an INI file with ExtendedInterpolation, then apply:
    21	      1) environment overrides: ORAC__SECTION__KEY=value
    22	      2) optional db overrides (apply_db_overrides())
    23	
    24	    Search order (first that exists wins) if config_file_path is None:
    25	      - resources/config/orac.ini
    26	      - ~/.config/orac/orac.ini (XDG)
    27	      - /etc/orac/orac.ini
    28	    """
    29	
    30	    def __init__(
    31	        self,
    32	        config_file_path: Optional[Path] = None,
    33	        *,
    34	        env_prefix: str = "ORAC__",
    35	        search_paths: Optional[Iterable[Path]] = None,
    36	    ):
    37	        self.env_prefix = env_prefix
    38	        self.search_paths: List[Path] = list(search_paths) if search_paths else [
    39	            Path("resources/config/orac.ini"),
    40	            Path(os.environ.get("XDG_CONFIG_HOME", Path.home() / ".config")) / "orac" / "orac.ini",
    41	            Path("/etc/orac/orac.ini"),
    42	        ]
    43	
    44	        # Resolve config file
    45	        if config_file_path is None:
    46	            for cand in self.search_paths:
    47	                if cand.exists():
    48	                    config_file_path = cand
    49	                    break
    50	        if config_file_path is None:
    51	            raise FileNotFoundError("No config file found in search paths.")
    52	        self.config_file_path = Path(config_file_path)
    53	
    54	        # Load. Keep ORAC_HOME available for ExtendedInterpolation so
    55	        # config values such as ${ORAC_HOME}/var can still be expanded
    56	        # through ConfigParser before normal path expansion.
    57	        self.config = ConfigParser(
    58	            defaults={
    59	                "ORAC_HOME": os.environ.get("ORAC_HOME", str(project_home())),
    60	            },
    61	            interpolation=ExtendedInterpolation(),
    62	        )
    63	        with open(self.config_file_path, encoding="utf-8") as f:
    64	            self.config.read_file(f)
    65	
    66	        # Apply environment overrides (ORAC__SECTION__KEY=value)
    67	        self._apply_env_overrides()
    68	
    69	        # Convenience: a flattened view (last one wins)
    70	        self.global_substitutions: Dict[str, str] = {}

    73	    # --------------------------- core helpers ---------------------------------
    74	    def _apply_env_overrides(self) -> None:
    75	        prefix = self.env_prefix
    76	        for k, v in os.environ.items():
    77	            if not k.startswith(prefix):
    78	                continue
    79	            try:
    80	                _, section, key = k.split("__", 2)
    81	            except ValueError:
    82	                # ignore badly-formed keys
    83	                continue
    84	            section = section.lower()
    85	            key = key.lower()
    86	            if not self.config.has_section(section):
    87	                self.config.add_section(section)
    88	            self.config.set(section, key, v)
    89	
    90	    def _hydrate_dictionary(self) -> None:
    91	        self.global_substitutions.clear()
    92	        for section in self.config.sections():
    93	            for key, val in self.config.items(section):
    94	                self.global_substitutions[key] = val  # note: simple flatten, last one wins

    99	            if default is not None:
   100	                return default

   102	        return self.config.get(section, key)

   106	            if default is not None:

   113	            if default is not None:

   116	        return self.config.getint(section, key)

   119	        if not self.config.has_section(section) or not self.config.has_option(section, key):
   120	            if default is not None:
   121	                return default
   122	            raise KeyError(f"Missing config key {section}.{key} in {self.config_file_path}")
   123	        return self.config.getfloat(section, key)

   126	        self, section: str, key: str, default: Optional[str] = None, suppress_warnings: bool = False
   127	    ) -> Path:
   128	        raw = self.config_value(section, key, default=default)
   129	        expanded = _expand_path(raw)
   130	        # warn if it doesn't look like a path (optional)
   131	        if not suppress_warnings and not (os.path.isabs(expanded) or any(sep in expanded for sep in ("/", "\\"))):
   132	            print(f"WARNING: expected a path for {section}.{key}, got '{raw}'")

   136	        val = self.config_value(section, key, default=default)

   141	        """
   142	        Apply overrides from the database. Expects an iterable of (section, key, value).
   143	        """
   144	        for section, key, value in kv_triples:

   146	            k = key.lower()

    22	class ApiKeyStore:

    24	
    25	  def __init__(self, *, store_path: Path | None = None) -> None:
    26	    """Create an API key store wrapper.

    30	    """
    31	    self.store_path = store_path or DEFAULT_API_KEY_STORE

    33	    if not self.store_path.exists():

    36	  def get_api_key(self, resource_name: str) -> str:
    37	    """Return the decrypted API key for a resource.
    38	
    39	    Args:

    41	        ``picovoice/access_key``.
    42	
    43	    Returns:

    45

    47	      ApiKeyStoreError: If the key is missing or cannot be decrypted.
    48	    """
    49	    section = _normalise_resource_name(resource_name)
    50	    config = self._read_config()
    51	    if not config.has_section(section) or not config.has_option(section, "api_key"):
    52	      raise ApiKeyStoreError(
    53	        f"API key resource '{section}' is not configured in "
    54	        f"{self.store_path}. Add it with: "
    55	        f"PYTHONPATH=src poetry run python -m lib.api_key_store "
    56	        f"--set {section}"

    58	
    59	    encrypted_value = config.get(section, "api_key")
    60	    try:
    61	      return decrypted_user_credential(encrypted_value)
    62	    except Exception as exc:
    63	      raise ApiKeyStoreError(
    64	        f"API key resource '{section}' exists but could not be decrypted on "
    65	        "this machine."
    66	      ) from exc

    68	  def set_api_key(self, resource_name: str, api_key: str) -> None:
    69	    """Store an encrypted API key for a resource.

    74	        storage and is never logged by this module.

    76	    section = _normalise_resource_name(resource_name)

    78	    if not config.has_section(section):
    79	      config.add_section(section)
    80	    config.set(section, "api_key", encrypted_user_credential(api_key))

    28	def _system_id():
    29	    """
    30	    Retrieves a unique system identifier based on the operating system.
    31	
    32	    This function detects the operating system and then executes appropriate
    33	    commands to fetch a unique identifier for the system. The method of fetching
    34	    the identifier varies based on whether the system is macOS (Darwin), Windows,
    35	    or Linux. If the operating system is not one of these, a default identifier
    36	    is returned.

    38	    Returns:
    39	        str: A unique system identifier or a default string if the OS is unsupported.
    40	    """
    41	    # Determine the operating system
    42	    operating_system = platform.system()
    43	
    44	    if operating_system == 'Darwin':
    45	        # macOS: Use ioreg and awk to fetch the IOPlatformUUID
    46	        command = "ioreg -d2 -c IOPlatformExpertDevice"
    47	        # Run ioreg command to get platform details
    48	        ioreg_cmd = subprocess.run(["ioreg", "-d2", "-c", "IOPlatformExpertDevice"],
    49	                                   stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE)
    50	        # Run awk command to extract the IOPlatformUUID
    51	        # Better?: awk_cmd = subprocess.run(["awk", '-F\"', "'/IOPlatformUUID/{print $(NF-1)}'"],
    52	        awk_cmd = subprocess.run(["awk", '-F\"', "'/IOPlatformUUID/{print $(NF-1)}'"],
    53	                                 input=ioreg_cmd.stdout, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE)
    54	        # Decode the output to get the system UID

    56	
    57	    elif operating_system == 'Windows':
    58	        # Windows: Use wmic to get the UUID
    59	        # Run PowerShell command to get the system UUID
    60	        system_uid = subprocess.run(["powershell", "(Get-CimInstance -Class Win32_ComputerSystemProduct).UUID"],

   480	def _data_encrypt(data_to_encrypt: str, encryption_password: str) -> str:
   481	    """
   482	    Encrypts the provided data using AES-256-GCM.
   483	
   484	    Args:
   485	        data_to_encrypt (str): The plaintext data to encrypt.
   486	        encryption_password (str): The password to derive the encryption key from.
   487	
   488	    Returns:
   489	        str: The base64-encoded encrypted data, including the salt, IV, tag, and ciphertext.
   490	    """
   491	    # Generate a random salt and IV
   492	    salt = os.urandom(16)
   493	    iv = os.urandom(12)
   494	
   495	    # Derive the encryption key using the password and salt
   496	    key = _derive_key(encryption_password, salt)
   497	
   498	    # Initialize the AES-GCM encryptor with the derived key and IV
   499	    encryptor = Cipher(
   500	        algorithms.AES(key),
   501	        modes.GCM(iv),
   502	        backend=default_backend()
   503	    ).encryptor()
   504	
   505	    # Encrypt the data
   506	    ciphertext = encryptor.update(data_to_encrypt.encode()) + encryptor.finalize()
   507	
   508	    # Concatenate the salt, IV, tag, and ciphertext, and base64-encode the result
   509	    encrypted_result = b64encode(salt + iv + encryptor.tag + ciphertext).decode('utf-8')
   510	    return encrypted_result

payload.message: On plugins, the manifest model and tests are fairly disciplined, but there is a split between routing/runtime and service lifecycle. `PluginServiceManager` exists and is tested, yet the main Orac startup path does not wire it in, while the Home Assistant manifest declares an auto-start hybrid service.

content.text: On plugins, the manifest model and tests are fairly disciplined, but there is a split between routing/runtime and service lifecycle. `PluginServiceManager` exists and is tested, yet the main Orac startup path does not wire it in, while the Home Assistant manifest declares an auto-start hybrid service.

   292	def _load_exit_phrases() -> set[str]:
   293	  """Load configured voice-session exit phrases.
   294	
   295	  Returns:
   296	    set[str]: Normalised phrases that should end a local voice session.
   297	  """
   298	  config_path = resolve_orac_home() / "resources" / "config" / "orac.ini"
   299	  config_mgr = ConfigManager(config_file_path=config_path)
   300	  configured = config_mgr.config_value(
   301	    "voice",
   302	    "session_exit_phrases",
   303	    default=",".join(DEFAULT_SESSION_EXIT_PHRASES),
   304	  )
   305	  phrases = {
   306	    phrase.strip().lower()
   307	    for phrase in configured.split(",")
   308	    if phrase.strip()
   309	  }
   310	  return phrases or set(DEFAULT_SESSION_EXIT_PHRASES)

   313	def _is_exit_phrase(text: str, exit_phrases: set[str]) -> bool:
   314	  """Return whether recognised or typed text should end the voice session."""
   315	  cleaned = text.strip().lower().strip(".!?;:")
   316	  return cleaned in exit_phrases

   319	def _load_record_mode(args: argparse.Namespace) -> str:
   320	  """Load the configured or overridden recording mode."""

   322	    return args.record_mode
   323	
   324	  config_path = resolve_orac_home() / "resources" / "config" / "orac.ini"
   325	  config_mgr = ConfigManager(config_file_path=config_path)
   326	  mode = config_mgr.config_value(

   331	  cleaned = mode.strip().lower()

   366	def _load_wake_rearm_seconds() -> float:
   367	  """Load the delay before wake listening resumes after a response."""
   368	  config_mgr = _voice_config_manager()

   381	def _create_barge_in_controller() -> BargeInController | OpenWakeWordBargeInController | None:
   382	  """Create the configured barge-in controller, if enabled."""
   383	  config_mgr = _voice_config_manager()
   384	  try:
   385	    config = load_barge_in_config(config_mgr)
   386	  except ValueError as exc:
   387	    raise VoiceActivationError(str(exc)) from exc
   388	  if not config.enable_experimental_barge_in:
   389	    return None
   390	  if config.mode == "vad":
   391	    logger.warning(VAD_BARGE_IN_EXPERIMENTAL_WARNING)
   392	    return BargeInController(config=config)
   393	  if config.mode == "wakeword":
   394	    logger.warning(WAKEWORD_BARGE_IN_EXPERIMENTAL_WARNING)
   395	    return OpenWakeWordBargeInController(config=config)
   396	  raise VoiceActivationError(f"Unsupported voice.barge_in_mode: {config.mode}")

   399	def _create_activation_listener(
   400	  *,
   401	  args: argparse.Namespace,
   402	  exit_phrases: set[str],
   403	  capture: SoundDeviceAudioCapture | None = None,
   404	  stt_engine: FasterWhisperSttEngine | None = None,
   405	) -> VoiceActivationListener:
   406	  """Create the configured local voice activation listener."""
   407	  activation_mode = _load_activation_mode(args)
   408	  if activation_mode == "enter":
   409	    return EnterActivationListener(exit_phrases=exit_phrases)
   410	
   411	  config_mgr = _voice_config_manager()
   412	  wake_engine = config_mgr.config_value(
   413	    "voice",
   414	    "wake_engine",
   415	    default=DEFAULT_WAKE_ENGINE,
   416	  )
   417	  wake_phrase = config_mgr.config_value(
   418	    "voice",
   419	    "wake_phrase",
   420	    default=DEFAULT_WAKE_PHRASE,
   421	  )
   422	  wake_model = config_mgr.config_value("voice", "wake_model", default="")
   423	  wake_threshold = float(
   424	    config_mgr.config_value("voice", "wake_threshold", default="0.6")
   425	  )
   426	  if activation_mode in {"openwakeword", "porcupine", "stt_phrase"}:
   427	    wake_engine = activation_mode
   428	
   429	  if wake_engine.strip().lower() in {"", "none"}:
   430	    raise VoiceActivationError(
   431	      "activation_mode=wake_word requires a supported wake_engine. "
   432	      "No wake-word engine is currently configured."
   433	    )
   434	  cleaned_wake_engine = wake_engine.strip().lower()
   435	  if cleaned_wake_engine == "openwakeword":
   436	    return OpenWakeWordActivationListener.from_config(
   437	      model_paths=config_mgr.config_value(
   438	        "voice",
   439	        "openwakeword_model_paths",
   440	        default="",
   441	      ),
   442	      model_names=config_mgr.config_value(
   443	        "voice",
   444	        "openwakeword_model_names",
   445	        default=",".join(DEFAULT_OPENWAKEWORD_MODEL_NAMES),
   446	      ),
   447	      model_dirs=config_mgr.config_value(
   448	        "voice",
   449	        "openwakeword_model_dirs",
   450	        default="",
   451	      ),
   452	      threshold=float(
   453	        config_mgr.config_value(
   454	          "voice",
   455	          "openwakeword_threshold",
   456	          default=str(DEFAULT_OPENWAKEWORD_THRESHOLD),
   457	        )
   458	      ),
   459	      inference_framework=config_mgr.config_value(
   460	        "voice",
   461	        "openwakeword_inference_framework",
   462	        default="auto",
   463	      ),
   464	      input_device=config_mgr.config_value(
   465	        "voice",
   466	        "stt_input_device",
   467	        default="default",
   468	      ),
   469	      frame_ms=int(
   470	        config_mgr.config_value(
   471	          "voice",
   472	          "wake_chunk_ms",
   473	          default=str(DEFAULT_OPENWAKEWORD_FRAME_MS),
   474	        )
   475	      ),
   476	      status_callback=_console_line,
   477	      refractory_seconds=float(
   478	        config_mgr.config_value(
   479	          "voice",
   480	          "openwakeword_refractory_seconds",
   481	          default=str(DEFAULT_OPENWAKEWORD_REFRACTORY_SECONDS),
   482	        )
   483	      ),
   484	    )
   485	  if cleaned_wake_engine == "porcupine":
   486	    return PorcupineActivationListener(
   487	      access_key_resource=config_mgr.config_value(
   488	        "voice",
   489	        "porcupine_access_key_resource",
   490	        default=DEFAULT_PORCUPINE_ACCESS_KEY_RESOURCE,
   491	      ),
   492	      keyword_path=config_mgr.config_value(
   493	        "voice",
   494	        "porcupine_keyword_path",
   495	        default="",
   496	      ),
   497	      builtin_keyword=config_mgr.config_value(
   498	        "voice",
   499	        "porcupine_builtin_keyword",
   500	        default="",
   501	      ),
   502	      sensitivity=float(
   503	        config_mgr.config_value(
   504	          "voice",
   505	          "porcupine_sensitivity",
   506	          default=str(wake_threshold),
   507	        )
   508	      ),
   509	    )
   510	  if cleaned_wake_engine == "stt_phrase":
   511	    if capture is None or stt_engine is None:
   512	      raise VoiceActivationError(
   513	        "wake_engine=stt_phrase requires local capture and STT components."
   514	      )
   515	    return SttPhraseWakeWordActivationListener(
   516	      wake_phrase=wake_phrase,
   517	      capture=capture,
   518	      stt_engine=stt_engine,
   519	      exit_phrases=exit_phrases,
   520	    )
   521	  return WakeWordActivationListener(
   522	    wake_engine=wake_engine,
   523	    wake_phrase=wake_phrase,
   524	    wake_model=wake_model,
   525	    wake_threshold=wake_threshold,
   526	  )

   529	def _run_tts_test(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
   530	  """Run the existing local TTS smoke test.
   531	
   532	  Args:
   533	    args (argparse.Namespace): Parsed CLI arguments.
   534	
   535	  Returns:
   536	    int: Process exit code.
   537	  """
   538	  try:
   539	    worker = create_local_tts_worker_from_config(
   540	      event_handler=_log_voice_event,
   541	      voice_name=args.voice,
   542	      voice_dir=args.voice_dir,
   543	    )
   544	    if worker is None:
   545	      logger.error("Voice output is disabled in orac.ini")
   546	      return 2
   547	
   548	    session_id = "local-voice-test"
   549	    turn_id = f"tts-test-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}"
   550	    worker.start()
   551	    queued = worker.enqueue_text(
   552	      session_id=session_id,
   553	      turn_id=turn_id,
   554	      text=args.tts_test,
   555	    )
   556	    if not queued:
   557	      logger.error("--tts-test must not be empty")
   558	      worker.stop(drain=False)
   559	      return 2
   560	
   561	    if not worker.wait_until_idle(timeout=args.wait_seconds):
   562	      logger.error("Timed out waiting for local voice output")
   563	      worker.stop(drain=False)
   564	      return 1
   565	
   566	    if worker.error_count:
   567	      message = worker.last_error.message if worker.last_error else "unknown"
   568	      logger.error("Local voice output failed: {}", message)
   569	      worker.stop(drain=True)
   570	      return 1
   571	
   572	    worker.stop(drain=True)
   573	    return 0
   574	  except Exception as exc:
   575	    logger.error("Local voice test failed: {}", exc)
   576	    return 1
   577

   579	def _transcribe_once(
   580	  args: argparse.Namespace,
   581	  *,
   582	  capture: SoundDeviceAudioCapture | None = None,
   583	  stt_engine: FasterWhisperSttEngine | None = None,
   584	  session_id: str | None = None,
   585	  prompt: str | None = "Press Enter to record speech.",
   586	  display_sender: DisplayEventSender | None = None,
   587	) -> tuple[str, str, str]:
   588	  """Capture and transcribe one local microphone sample.
   589	
   590	  Args:
   591	    args (argparse.Namespace): Parsed CLI arguments.
   592	    capture (SoundDeviceAudioCapture | None): Optional reusable capture layer.
   593	    stt_engine (FasterWhisperSttEngine | None): Optional reusable STT engine.
   594	    session_id (str | None): Optional stable voice session id.
   595	    prompt (str): Prompt shown before recording.
   596	    display_sender (DisplayEventSender | None): Optional display event sender.
   597	
   598	  Returns:
   599	    tuple[str, str, str]: Session id, turn id, and recognised text.
   600	  """
   601	  session_id = session_id or f"local-voice-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}"
   602	  turn_id = f"stt-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}"
   603	  capture = capture or SoundDeviceAudioCapture.from_config(
   604	    record_seconds=args.record_seconds
   605	  )
   606	  stt_engine = stt_engine or FasterWhisperSttEngine.from_config()
   607	  record_mode = _load_record_mode(args)
   608	  timing_started_at = time.perf_counter()
   609	  record_started_at: float | None = None
   610	  record_finished_at: float | None = None
   611	  transcribe_started_at: float | None = None
   612	  transcribe_finished_at: float | None = None
   613	
   614	  if prompt is not None:
   615	    entered = input(prompt)
   616	    if _is_exit_phrase(entered, _load_exit_phrases()):
   617	      raise EOFError
   618	  
   619	  if display_sender:
   620	    _send_display_event(
   621	      display_sender,
   622	      "transcript.turn.clear",
   623	      session_id=session_id,
   624	      turn_id=turn_id,
   625	    )
   626	    display_sender.send_state(
   627	      "listening",
   628	      message="Listening...",
   629	      session_id=session_id,
   630	      turn_id=turn_id
   631	    )
   632	
   633	  _emit(
   634	    VoiceSttStarted(
   635	      session_id=session_id,
   636	      turn_id=turn_id,
   637	      record_seconds=(
   638	        capture.record_seconds if record_mode == "fixed" else None
   639	      ),
   640	    )
   641	  )
   642	  try:
   643	    record_started_at = time.perf_counter()
   644	    if record_mode == "vad":
   645	      wav_path = _record_vad_sample(
   646	        capture=capture,
   647	        session_id=session_id,
   648	        turn_id=turn_id,
   649	      )
   650	    else:
   651	      wav_path = capture.record_to_wav(
   652	        session_id=session_id,
   653	        turn_id=turn_id,
   654	        record_seconds=args.record_seconds,
   655	      )
   656	    record_finished_at = time.perf_counter()
   657	    _emit(
   658	      VoiceSttEnded(
   659	        session_id=session_id,
   660	        turn_id=turn_id,
   661	        wav_path=wav_path,
   662	      )
   663	    )
   664	
   665	    if display_sender:
   666	      display_sender.send_state(
   667	        "transcribing",
   668	        message="Transcribing...",
   669	        session_id=session_id,
   670	        turn_id=turn_id
   671	      )
   672	
   673	    transcribe_started_at = time.perf_counter()
   674	    recognised_text = stt_engine.transcribe_wav(wav_path)
   675	    transcribe_finished_at = time.perf_counter()
   676	    _emit(
   677	      VoiceSttFinal(
   678	        session_id=session_id,
   679	        turn_id=turn_id,
   680	        text=recognised_text,
   681	      )
   682	    )
   683	    logger.info(
   684	      (
   685	        "Voice STT timing: session={} turn={} mode={} record={:.2f}s "
   686	        "transcribe={:.2f}s total={:.2f}s"
   687	      ),
   688	      session_id,
   689	      turn_id,
   690	      record_mode,
   691	      (
   692	        record_finished_at - record_started_at
   693	        if record_started_at is not None and record_finished_at is not None
   694	        else 0.0
   695	      ),
   696	      (
   697	        transcribe_finished_at - transcribe_started_at
   698	        if (
   699	          transcribe_started_at is not None
   700	          and transcribe_finished_at is not None
   701	        )
   702	        else 0.0
   703	      ),
   704	      transcribe_finished_at - timing_started_at,
   705	    )
   706	    _send_display_event(
   707	      display_sender,
   708	      "transcript.user.final",
   709	      session_id=session_id,
   710	      turn_id=turn_id,
   711	      text=recognised_text,
   712	    )
   713	    return session_id, turn_id, recognised_text
   714	  except KeyboardInterrupt:
   715	    capture.cancel()
   716	    raise
   717	  except Exception as exc:
   718	    _emit(
   719	      VoiceSttError(
   720	        session_id=session_id,
   721	        turn_id=turn_id,
   722	        message=str(exc),
   723	      )
   724	    )
   725	    raise

   728	def _record_vad_sample(
   729	  *,
   730	  capture: SoundDeviceAudioCapture,
   731	  session_id: str,
   732	  turn_id: str,
   733	) -> Path:
   734	  """Record one VAD-controlled utterance and return its WAV path."""
   735	  last_status: set[str] = set()
   736	
   737	  def _status(label: str) -> None:
   738	    if label == "listening" and label not in last_status:
   739	      last_status.add(label)
   740	      _console_line("Listening...")
   741	      _emit(
   742	        VoiceVadListeningStarted(
   743	          session_id=session_id,
   744	          turn_id=turn_id,
   745	          initial_timeout_seconds=(
   746	            capture.vad_config.initial_timeout_seconds
   747	          ),
   748	        )
   749	      )
   750	    elif label == "speech_started" and label not in last_status:
   751	      last_status.add(label)
   752	      _console_line("Speech detected...")
   753	      _emit(VoiceVadSpeechStarted(session_id=session_id, turn_id=turn_id))
   754	    elif label in {"speech_ended", "max_duration_reached"}:
   755	      _console_line("Speech ended; transcribing...")
   756	
   757	  result = capture.record_until_silence_to_wav(
   758	    session_id=session_id,
   759	    turn_id=turn_id,
   760	    status_callback=_status,

Chunk ID: 764b21

Process exited with code 0
Original token count: 5696
Output:
   760	    status_callback=_status,
   761	  )
   762	  if result.no_speech_timeout:
   763	    _emit(
   764	      VoiceVadTimeout(
   765	        session_id=session_id,
   766	        turn_id=turn_id,
   767	        initial_timeout_seconds=capture.vad_config.initial_timeout_seconds,
   768	      )
   769	    )
   770	    raise NoSpeechDetectedError("No speech detected before timeout")
   771	  if result.wav_path is None:
   772	    raise NoSpeechDetectedError("No usable speech was captured")
   773	
   774	  _emit(
   775	    VoiceVadSpeechEnded(
   776	      session_id=session_id,
   777	      turn_id=turn_id,
   778	      duration_seconds=result.duration_seconds,
   779	      max_duration_reached=result.max_duration_reached,
   780	    )
   781	  )
   782	  return result.wav_path

   785	async def _send_orac_prompt(
   786	  *,
   787	  reader: asyncio.StreamReader,
   788	  writer: asyncio.StreamWriter,
   789	  prompt_text: str,

   141	class LiveKitAcousticEchoCanceller:
   142	  """AEC adapter backed by LiveKit's WebRTC AudioProcessingModule.
   143	
   144	  The adapter maps Orac's fixed 10 ms mono int16 PCM frame contract onto
   145	  LiveKit's Python-side APM API. LiveKit mutates ``AudioFrame`` instances
   146	  in place, so capture processing returns a copy of the processed frame.
   147	  """
   148	
   149	  def __init__(
   150	    self,
   151	    *,
   152	    stream_delay_ms: int = DEFAULT_AEC_STREAM_DELAY_MS,
   153	    apm_factory: LiveKitApmFactory | None = None,
   154	    audio_frame_factory: LiveKitAudioFrameFactory | None = None,
   155	  ) -> None:
   156	    """Initialise the LiveKit AEC adapter.
   157	
   158	    Args:
   159	      stream_delay_ms (int): Delay between reverse and capture streams.
   160	      apm_factory (LiveKitApmFactory | None): Optional factory used by
   161	        tests to supply a LiveKit-compatible APM.
   162	      audio_frame_factory (LiveKitAudioFrameFactory | None): Optional
   163	        factory used by tests to supply LiveKit-compatible frames.
   164	
   165	    Raises:
   166	      RuntimeError: If LiveKit is unavailable or lacks the required API.
   167	      ValueError: If ``stream_delay_ms`` is negative.
   168	    """
   169	    if stream_delay_ms < 0:
   170	      raise ValueError("voice.aec_stream_delay_ms must be zero or greater")
   171	
   172	    self.stream_delay_ms = stream_delay_ms
   173	    self._apm_factory = apm_factory or _load_livekit_apm_factory()
   174	    self._audio_frame_factory = (
   175	      audio_frame_factory or _load_livekit_audio_frame_factory()
   176	    )
   177	    self._apm = self._create_apm()
   178	
   179	  def process_reverse_frame(self, frame: bytes) -> None:
   180	    """Feed one playback reference frame into LiveKit APM.
   181	
   182	    Args:
   183	      frame (bytes): Exact 10 ms mono int16 16 kHz PCM frame.
   184	    """
   185	    validate_aec_frame(frame, label="AEC reverse frame")
   186	    audio_frame = self._create_audio_frame(frame)
   187	    self._apm.process_reverse_stream(audio_frame)
   188	
   189	  def process_capture_frame(self, frame: bytes) -> bytes:
   190	    """Process one microphone capture frame through LiveKit APM.
   191	
   192	    Args:
   193	      frame (bytes): Exact 10 ms mono int16 16 kHz PCM frame.
   194	
   195	    Returns:
   196	      bytes: Echo-processed capture frame in Orac's AEC format.
   197	    """
   198	    validate_aec_frame(frame, label="AEC capture frame")
   199	    audio_frame = self._create_audio_frame(frame)
   200	    self._apm.process_stream(audio_frame)
   201	    processed = _audio_frame_bytes(audio_frame)
   202	    validate_aec_frame(processed, label="AEC processed capture frame")
   203	    return processed
   204	
   205	  def reset(self) -> None:
   206	    """Reset LiveKit APM state by recreating the underlying module."""
   207	    self._apm = self._create_apm()
   208	
   209	  def _create_apm(self) -> Any:
   210	    """Create and configure a LiveKit APM instance."""
   211	    apm = self._apm_factory()
   212	    apm.set_stream_delay_ms(self.stream_delay_ms)
   213	    return apm
   214	
   215	  def _create_audio_frame(self, frame: bytes) -> Any:
   216	    """Create one mutable LiveKit ``AudioFrame`` from Orac PCM bytes."""
   217	    return self._audio_frame_factory(
   218	      bytearray(frame),
   219	      AEC_SAMPLE_RATE,
   220	      AEC_CHANNELS,
   221	      AEC_SAMPLES_PER_FRAME,
   222	    )
   223	
   224	
   225	def _load_livekit_apm_factory() -> LiveKitApmFactory:
   226	  """Load LiveKit's APM constructor if the required API is present.
   227	
   228	  Returns:
   229	    LiveKitApmFactory: Factory creating an echo-cancelling APM.
   230	
   231	  Raises:
   232	    RuntimeError: If the LiveKit package or required API is unavailable.
   233	  """
   234	  try:
   235	    from livekit.rtc import AudioProcessingModule
   236	  except (ImportError, AttributeError) as exc:
   237	    raise RuntimeError(_LIVEKIT_IMPORT_ERROR) from exc
   238	
   239	  def _factory() -> Any:
   240	    return AudioProcessingModule(echo_cancellation=True)
   241	
   242	  return _factory
   243	
   244	
   245	def _load_livekit_audio_frame_factory() -> LiveKitAudioFrameFactory:
   246	  """Load LiveKit's ``AudioFrame`` constructor.
   247	
   248	  Returns:
   249	    LiveKitAudioFrameFactory: Factory creating LiveKit audio frames.
   250	
   251	  Raises:
   252	    RuntimeError: If the LiveKit package or required API is unavailable.
   253	  """
   254	  try:
   255	    from livekit.rtc import AudioFrame
   256	  except (ImportError, AttributeError) as exc:
   257	    raise RuntimeError(_LIVEKIT_IMPORT_ERROR) from exc
   258	  return AudioFrame
   259	
   260	
   261	def _audio_frame_bytes(audio_frame: Any) -> bytes:
   262	  """Extract raw int16 bytes from a LiveKit-compatible audio frame.
   263	
   264	  Args:
   265	    audio_frame (Any): LiveKit ``AudioFrame`` or test double.
   266	
   267	  Returns:
   268	    bytes: Raw PCM bytes.
   269	
   270	  Raises:
   271	    RuntimeError: If the frame does not expose readable audio bytes.
   272	  """
   273	  data = getattr(audio_frame, "data", None)
   274	  if data is not None:
   275	    try:
   276	      return memoryview(data).cast("B").tobytes()
   277	    except TypeError:
   278	      return bytes(data)
   279	
   280	  raw_data = getattr(audio_frame, "_data", None)
   281	  if raw_data is not None:
   282	    return bytes(raw_data)
   283	
   284	  raise RuntimeError("LiveKit AudioFrame did not expose processed audio data")

   287	def create_aec_backend(
   288	  *,
   289	  backend_name: str = DEFAULT_AEC_BACKEND,
   290	  stream_delay_ms: int = DEFAULT_AEC_STREAM_DELAY_MS,
   291	) -> AcousticEchoCanceller:
   292	  """Create an Orac AEC backend by name.
   293	
   294	  Args:
   295	    backend_name (str): Configured backend name.
   296	    stream_delay_ms (int): Delay between playback reference and capture.
   297	
   298	  Returns:
   299	    AcousticEchoCanceller: Configured AEC adapter.
   300	
   301	  Raises:
   302	    ValueError: If the backend name is unsupported.
   303	  """
   304	  cleaned = backend_name.strip().lower()
   305	  if cleaned == DEFAULT_AEC_BACKEND:
   306	    return NullAcousticEchoCanceller()
   307	  if cleaned == LIVEKIT_AEC_BACKEND:
   308	    return LiveKitAcousticEchoCanceller(stream_delay_ms=stream_delay_ms)
   309	  raise ValueError(
   310	    "Unsupported voice.aec_backend: "
   311	    f"{backend_name}. Supported values: "
   312	    f"{', '.join(sorted(SUPPORTED_AEC_BACKENDS))}"
   313	  )
   314	
   315	
   316	def create_aec_adapter_from_config(
   317	  config_mgr: ConfigManager,
   318	) -> AcousticEchoCanceller:
   319	  """Create the configured backend-neutral AEC adapter.
   320	
   321	  Args:
   322	    config_mgr (ConfigManager): Orac configuration manager.
   323	
   324	  Returns:
   325	    AcousticEchoCanceller: Configured AEC adapter.
   326	
   327	  Raises:
   328	    ValueError: If the backend config is unsupported.
   329	  """
   330	  backend_name = config_mgr.config_value(
   331	    "voice",
   332	    "aec_backend",
   333	    default=DEFAULT_AEC_BACKEND,
   334	  ).strip().lower()
   335	  stream_delay_ms = config_mgr.int_config_value(
   336	    "voice",
   337	    "aec_stream_delay_ms",
   338	    default=DEFAULT_AEC_STREAM_DELAY_MS,
   339	  )
   340	  backend = create_aec_backend(
   341	    backend_name=backend_name,
   342	    stream_delay_ms=stream_delay_ms,
   343	  )
   344	  logger.info(
   345	    "AEC backend selected: {} stream_delay_ms={}",
   346	    backend_name,
   347	    stream_delay_ms,
   348	  )
   349	  return backend

   119	class _CaptureAecProcessor:
   120	  """Apply capture-side AEC processing to exact 10 ms PCM frames."""
   121	
   122	  def __init__(
   123	    self,
   124	    *,
   125	    aec_adapter: AcousticEchoCanceller | None,
   126	    sample_rate: int,
   127	  ) -> None:
   128	    """Initialise a capture-side AEC frame processor.
   129	
   130	    Args:
   131	      aec_adapter (AcousticEchoCanceller | None): Optional AEC adapter.
   132	      sample_rate (int): Capture sample rate in hertz.
   133	    """
   134	    self.aec_adapter = aec_adapter or NullAcousticEchoCanceller()
   135	    self.sample_rate = sample_rate
   136	    self.enabled = (
   137	      aec_adapter is not None
   138	      and not isinstance(aec_adapter, NullAcousticEchoCanceller)
   139	    )
   140	    self._pending = bytearray()
   141	
   142	  def reset(self) -> None:
   143	    """Reset buffered capture and adapter state."""
   144	    self._pending.clear()
   145	    self.aec_adapter.reset()
   146	
   147	  def process_int16_bytes(self, frame_bytes: bytes) -> bytes:
   148	    """Process int16 capture bytes through the configured AEC adapter.
   149	
   150	    Args:
   151	      frame_bytes (bytes): Mono int16 PCM bytes.
   152	
   153	    Returns:
   154	      bytes: AEC-processed mono int16 PCM bytes.
   155	
   156	    Raises:
   157	      RuntimeError: If capture format or adapter output is invalid.
   158	    """
   159	    if not self.enabled:
   160	      return frame_bytes
   161	    if self.sample_rate != AEC_SAMPLE_RATE:
   162	      raise RuntimeError(
   163	        f"Capture-side AEC requires {AEC_SAMPLE_RATE} Hz audio"
   164	      )
   165	
   166	    self._pending.extend(frame_bytes)
   167	    processed = bytearray()
   168	    while len(self._pending) >= AEC_BYTES_PER_FRAME:
   169	      frame = bytes(self._pending[:AEC_BYTES_PER_FRAME])
   170	      del self._pending[:AEC_BYTES_PER_FRAME]
   171	      validate_aec_frame(frame, label="AEC capture frame")
   172	      cleaned = self.aec_adapter.process_capture_frame(frame)
   173	      validate_aec_frame(cleaned, label="AEC processed capture frame")
   174	      processed.extend(cleaned)
   175	    return bytes(processed)
   176	
   177	  def flush(self) -> bytes:
   178	    """Flush any trailing capture bytes, processing only complete frames.
   179	
   180	    Returns:
   181	      bytes: Unprocessed trailing bytes that did not form a full AEC frame.
   182	    """
   183	    if not self.enabled:
   184	      return b""
   185	    remainder = bytes(self._pending)
   186	    self._pending.clear()
   187	    return remainder

    46	class LocalAudioPlayback:
    47	  """Play WAV files on the local Linux desktop.
    48	
    49	  The command selection is intentionally isolated so it can later be
    50	  replaced with a Raspberry Pi streaming endpoint.
    51	  """
    52	
    53	  def __init__(
    54	    self,
    55	    *,
    56	    player: str | None = None,
    57	    timeout_seconds: int = DEFAULT_PLAYBACK_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
    58	  ) -> None:
    59	    """Initialise local playback.
    60	
    61	    Args:
    62	      player (str | None): Optional command override.
    63	      timeout_seconds (int): Playback timeout.
    64	    """
    65	    self.timeout_seconds = timeout_seconds
    66	    self.players = [player] if player else self._detect_players()
    67	    self.player = self.players[0]
    68	    self._process_lock = threading.Lock()
    69	    self._active_process: subprocess.Popen[str] | None = None
    70	
    71	  def _detect_players(self) -> list[str]:
    72	    """Return supported WAV players in preference order.
    73	
    74	    Returns:
    75	      list[str]: Player executable names.
    76	
    77	    Raises:
    78	      RuntimeError: If no supported player is available.
    79	    """
    80	    players: list[str] = []
    81	    for candidate in ("paplay", "aplay", "ffplay"):
    82	      if shutil.which(candidate):
    83	        players.append(candidate)
    84	    if not players:
    85	      raise RuntimeError("No supported audio player found: paplay, aplay, ffplay")
    86	    return players
    87	
    88	  def _command_for(self, *, player: str, wav_path: Path) -> list[str]:
    89	    """Build the playback command for the selected player.
    90	
    91	    Args:
    92	      player (str): Player executable.
    93	      wav_path (Path): WAV file path.
    94	
    95	    Returns:
    96	      list[str]: Command arguments.
    97	    """
    98	    if player == "ffplay":
    99	      return [
   100	        player,
   101	        "-nodisp",
   102	        "-autoexit",
   103	        "-loglevel",
   104	        "error",
   105	        str(wav_path),
   106	      ]
   107	    return [player, str(wav_path)]
   108	
   109	  def play_wav(self, wav_path: Path) -> None:
   110	    """Play a WAV file locally.
   111	
   112	    Args:
   113	      wav_path (Path): Path to a WAV file.
   114	
   115	    Raises:
   116	      FileNotFoundError: If the WAV file does not exist.
   117	      RuntimeError: If playback fails.
   118	    """
   119	    if not wav_path.exists():
   120	      raise FileNotFoundError(f"WAV file does not exist: {wav_path}")
   121	
   122	    failures: list[str] = []
   123	    for player in self.players:
   124	      command = self._command_for(player=player, wav_path=wav_path)
   125	      logger.debug("Playing generated voice WAV with {}", player)
   126	      process: subprocess.Popen[str] | None = None
   127	      try:
   128	        process = subprocess.Popen(
   129	          command,
   130	          stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL,
   131	          stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
   132	          stderr=subprocess.PIPE,
   133	          text=True,
   134	        )
   135	        with self._process_lock:
   136	          self._active_process = process
   137	
   138	        stdout, stderr = process.communicate(
   139	          timeout=self.timeout_seconds,
   140	        )
   141	        if process.returncode != 0:
   142	          raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(
   143	            process.returncode,
   144	            command,
   145	            output=stdout,
   146	            stderr=stderr,
   147	          )
   148	        self.player = player
   149	        return
   150	      except subprocess.CalledProcessError as exc:
   151	        message = self._summarise_failure(exc.stderr or exc.stdout or "")
   152	        failures.append(f"{player}: {message}")
   153	      except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
   154	        self._terminate_process(process, reason="playback timed out")
   155	        failures.append(f"{player}: playback timed out")
   156	      finally:
   157	        with self._process_lock:
   158	          if self._active_process is process:
   159	            self._active_process = None
   160	
   161	    raise RuntimeError("Audio playback failed: " + " | ".join(failures))
   162	
   163	  def cancel(self) -> None:
   164	    """Terminate any active local playback process."""
   165	    with self._process_lock:
   166	      process = self._active_process
   167	    self._terminate_process(process, reason="cancellation requested")
   168	
   169	  def _terminate_process(
   170	    self,
   171	    process: subprocess.Popen[str] | None,
   172	    *,
   173	    reason: str,
   174	  ) -> None:
   175	    """Terminate a playback process if it is still running."""
   176	    if process is None or process.poll() is not None:
   177	      return
   178	    logger.info("Terminating audio playback process: {}", reason)
   179	    process.terminate()
   180	    try:
   181	      process.wait(timeout=1.0)
   182	    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
   183	      logger.warning("Killing unresponsive audio playback process")
   184	      process.kill()
   185	      process.wait(timeout=1.0)
   186	
   187	  @staticmethod
   188	  def _summarise_failure(output: str, *, limit: int = 600) -> str:
   189	    """Return a bounded single-line playback failure summary."""
   190	    cleaned = " ".join((output or "").strip().split())
   191	    if not cleaned:
   192	      return "command failed"
   193	    if len(cleaned) <= limit:
   194	      return cleaned
   195	    return cleaned[:limit].rstrip() + "..."

   198	class NativeAudioPlayback:
   199	  """Play WAV files through ``sounddevice`` while exposing PCM frames.
   200	
   201	  The native backend is experimental and exists so future AEC wiring can
   202	  receive the exact playback PCM frames before they are sent to the output
   203	  device.
   204	  """
   205	
   206	  def __init__(
   207	    self,
   208	    *,
   209	    on_playback_frame: PlaybackFrameHandler | None = None,
   210	    frame_ms: int = DEFAULT_NATIVE_PLAYBACK_FRAME_MS,
   211	    timeout_seconds: int = DEFAULT_PLAYBACK_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
   212	    device: str | int | None = None,
   213	    sounddevice_module=None,
   214	  ) -> None:
   215	    """Initialise the experimental native playback backend.
   216	
   217	    Args:
   218	      on_playback_frame (PlaybackFrameHandler | None): Optional PCM hook.
   219	      frame_ms (int): Target frame duration in milliseconds.
   220	      timeout_seconds (int): Maximum playback time before cancellation.
   221	      device (str | int | None): Optional sounddevice output device.
   222	      sounddevice_module: Optional dependency injection hook for tests.
   223	    """
   224	    self.on_playback_frame = on_playback_frame
   225	    self.frame_ms = frame_ms
   226	    self.timeout_seconds = timeout_seconds
   227	    self.device = device
   228	    self._sounddevice = sounddevice_module
   229	    self._process_lock = threading.Lock()
   230	    self._active_stream = None
   231	    self._cancel_requested = threading.Event()
   232	
   233	  def set_playback_frame_handler(
   234	    self,
   235	    handler: PlaybackFrameHandler | None,
   236	  ) -> None:
   237	    """Set or clear the PCM hook used for playback frame inspection.
   238	
   239	    Args:
   240	      handler (PlaybackFrameHandler | None): Optional PCM hook.
   241	    """
   242	    self.on_playback_frame = handler
   243	
   244	  def _load_sounddevice(self):
   245	    """Return the ``sounddevice`` module or fail clearly.
   246	
   247	    Returns:
   248	      module: Imported ``sounddevice`` module.
   249	
   250	    Raises:
   251	      RuntimeError: If the dependency is missing.
   252	    """
   253	    if self._sounddevice is not None:
   254	      return self._sounddevice
   255	    try:
   256	      self._sounddevice = importlib.import_module("sounddevice")
   257	    except ImportError as exc:
   258	      raise RuntimeError(
   259	        "playback_backend=native requires the sounddevice package"
   260	      ) from exc
   261	    return self._sounddevice
   262	
   263	  def play_wav(self, wav_path: Path) -> None:
   264	    """Play a WAV file through the native PCM path.
   265	
   266	    Args:
   267	      wav_path (Path): Path to a WAV file.
   268	
   269	    Raises:
   270	      FileNotFoundError: If the WAV file does not exist.
   271	      RuntimeError: If playback fails or is cancelled.
   272	    """
   273	    if not wav_path.exists():
   274	      raise FileNotFoundError(f"WAV file does not exist: {wav_path}")
   275	
   276	    sd = self._load_sounddevice()
   277	    self._cancel_requested.clear()
   278	    logger.debug("Playing generated voice WAV with native sounddevice backend")
   279	
   280	    with wave.open(str(wav_path), "rb") as wav_file:
   281	      channels = wav_file.getnchannels()
   282	      sample_width = wav_file.getsampwidth()
   283	      sample_rate = wav_file.getframerate()
   284	      frame_samples = max(1, round(sample_rate * self.frame_ms / 1000))
   285	      frame_bytes = frame_samples * channels * sample_width
   286	      logger.info(
   287	        (
   288	          "Native playback backend selected: sample_rate={} channels={} "
   289	          "sample_width={} frame_samples={} frame_bytes={} device={}"
   290	        ),
   291	        sample_rate,
   292	        channels,
   293	        sample_width,
   294	        frame_samples,
   295	        frame_bytes,
   296	        self.device,
   297	      )
   298	      if self.on_playback_frame is not None:
   299	        logger.debug("Native playback frame hook configured")
   300	
   301	      stream = sd.RawOutputStream(
   302	        samplerate=sample_rate,
   303	        channels=channels,
   304	        dtype=self._dtype_for_sample_width(sample_width),
   305	        blocksize=frame_samples,
   306	        device=self.device,
   307	      )
   308	      deadline = time.monotonic() + self.timeout_seconds if self.timeout_seconds else None
   309	      with stream:
   310	        with self._process_lock:
   311	          self._active_stream = stream
   312	        try:
   313	          first_frame_logged = False
   314	          while not self._cancel_requested.is_set():
   315	            if deadline is not None and time.monotonic() > deadline:
   316	              raise RuntimeError("Audio playback timed out")
   317	            chunk = wav_file.readframes(frame_samples)
   318	            if not chunk:
   319	              break
   320	            if self.on_playback_frame is not None:
   321	              self.on_playback_frame(chunk, sample_rate, channels, sample_width)
   322	            if not first_frame_logged:
   323	              logger.debug(
   324	                "Native playback frame hook invoked: sample_rate={} channels={} sample_width={} frame_bytes={}",
   325	                sample_rate,
   326	                channels,
   327	                sample_width,
   328	                len(chunk),
   329	              )
   330	              first_frame_logged = True
   331	            stream.write(chunk)
   332	          if self._cancel_requested.is_set():
   333	            raise RuntimeError("Audio playback cancelled")
   334	        finally:
   335	          with self._process_lock:
   336	            if self._active_stream is stream:
   337	              self._active_stream = None
   338	
   339	  def cancel(self) -> None:
   340	    """Cancel native playback if a stream is active."""
   341	    self._cancel_requested.set()
   342	    with self._process_lock:
   343	      stream = self._active_stream
   344	    if stream is None:
   345	      return
   346	    logger.info("Terminating native audio playback stream: cancellation requested")
   347	    try:
   348	      stream.abort()
   349	    except Exception as exc:  # pragma: no cover - defensive cleanup
   350	      logger.debug("Native playback abort request failed: {}", exc)
   351	
   352	  @staticmethod
   353	  def _dtype_for_sample_width(sample_width: int) -> str:
   354	    """Return a ``sounddevice`` dtype for one PCM sample width."""
   355	    if sample_width == 1:
   356	      return "uint8"
   357	    if sample_width == 2:
   358	      return "int16"
   359	    if sample_width == 4:
   360	      return "int32"
   361	    raise RuntimeError(f"Unsupported PCM sample width for native playback: {sample_width}")

    64	  """TTS engine wrapper that falls back when the primary engine fails."""
    65	
    66	  def __init__(
    67	    self,
    68	    *,
    69	    primary: TtsEngine,
    70	    fallback: TtsEngine,
    71	    primary_name: str,
    72	    fallback_name: str,

    74	    """Initialise fallback synthesis.
    75	
    76	    Args:
    77	      primary (TtsEngine): Preferred synthesis backend.
    78	      fallback (TtsEngine): Backup synthesis backend.
    79	      primary_name (str): Human-readable primary backend name.
    80	      fallback_name (str): Human-readable fallback backend name.
    81	    """
    82	    self.primary = primary
    83	    self.fallback = fallback
    84	    self.primary_name = primary_name
    85	    self.fallback_name = fallback_name
    86	
    87	  def synthesise_to_wav(
    88	    self,
    89	    text: str,
    90	    *,
    91	    session_id: str,
    92	    turn_id: str,
    93	  ) -> Path:
    94	    """Synthesise with the primary engine, then fallback on failure."""
    95	    try:
    96	      return self.primary.synthesise_to_wav(
    97	        text,
    98	        session_id=session_id,
    99	        turn_id=turn_id,
   100	      )
   101	    except Exception as exc:
   102	      logger.warning(
   103	        "TTS backend '{}' failed; falling back to '{}': {}",
   104	        self.primary_name,

   109	        text,

   111	        turn_id=turn_id,
   112	      )
   113	
   114	  def cancel(self) -> None:
   115	    """Cancel active synthesis in both engines where supported."""
   116	    for engine in (self.primary, self.fallback):
   117	      cancel = getattr(engine, "cancel", None)
   118	      if callable(cancel):

   282	    total = self.current_turn_frames_emitted + emitted
   283	    self.last_completed_session_id = session_id
   284	    self.last_completed_turn_id = turn_id
   285	    self.last_completed_frames_emitted = total
   286	    self.last_completed_reason = reason
   287	    logger.info(
   288	      (
   289	        "Playback reference turn completed: session={} turn={} "
   290	        "frames_emitted={} reason={}"
   291	      ),
   292	      session_id,
   293	      turn_id,
   294	      total,
   295	      reason,
   296	    )
   297	    self._aec_adapter.reset()
   298	    self._clear_active_turn()
   299	    return emitted
   300	
   301	  def cancel_turn(
   302	    self,
   303	    *,
   304	    session_id: str,
   305	    turn_id: str,
   306	    reason: str = "cancelled",
   307	  ) -> int:
   308	    """Cancel the active playback turn and discard buffered reference PCM.
   309	
   310	    Args:
   311	      session_id (str): Session identifier.
   312	      turn_id (str): Turn identifier.
   313	      reason (str): Cancellation reason.
   314	
   315	    Returns:
   316	      int: Number of discarded reference frames.
   317	    """
   318	    if not self._matches_active_turn(session_id=session_id, turn_id=turn_id):
   319	      return 0
   320	    discarded = self.current_turn_frames_emitted
   321	    self.last_cancelled_session_id = session_id
   322	    self.last_cancelled_turn_id = turn_id
   323	    self.last_cancelled_frames_emitted = discarded
   324	    self.last_cancelled_reason = reason
   325	    logger.info(
   326	      (
   327	        "Playback reference turn cancelled: session={} turn={} "
   328	        "frames_emitted={} reason={}"
   329	      ),
   330	      session_id,
   331	      turn_id,
   332	      discarded,
   333	      reason,
   334	    )
   335	    self._resampler.reset()
   336	    self._aec_adapter.reset()
   337	    self._clear_active_turn()
   338	    return discarded
   339	
   340	  def reset(self, *, reason: str = "reset") -> None:
   341	    """Discard any active playback turn without finalising it."""
   342	    if self.current_turn_id is None:
   343	      return
   344	    logger.debug(
   345	      "Playback reference turn reset: session={} turn={} reason={}",
   346	      self.current_session_id,
   347	      self.current_turn_id,
   348	      reason,
   349	    )
   350	    self._resampler.reset()
   351	    self._aec_adapter.reset()
   352	    self._clear_active_turn()
   353	
   354	  def _matches_active_turn(self, *, session_id: str, turn_id: str) -> bool:
   355	    """Return whether the supplied turn matches the active playback turn."""
   356	    return (
   357	      self.current_session_id == session_id
   358	      and self.current_turn_id == turn_id
   359	    )
   360
